name: Beef Pita
recipeInstructions:
Combine ground beef, onion, and garlic in a large frying pan; cook, stirring frequently until the beef loses its pink color. Pour off drippings. Add mushrooms, bay leaf, salt, chili powder, cumin powder, and cinnamon; cook, stirring frequently, until mushrooms are tender about 5 minutes. Stir in tomato sauce and wine; cook, covered, for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Remove the bay leaf. Cool while preparing cheese filling. Combine egg and cream cheese in a medium bowl; beat with an electric mixer until smooth. Stir in cottage cheese and feta cheese and blend.
Brush the baking pan (13 x 9-inch) with melted butter. Line the pan with one sheet of pastry, fitting the pastry to the contour of the pan. Brush the pastry with butter. Layer with three more pastry sheets, brushing each with butter.
Sprinkle bread crumbs evenly over top. Spoon about 1/5 of the meat filling in a layer over the crumbs and about 1/5 of the cheese filling over the meat. Place one pastry sheet over the cheese filling, crinkling to fit inside the dimensions of the pan; brush with butter and layer with about 1/5 of the meat and about 1/5 of the cheese fillings.
Repeat with three more pastry sheets, crinkling each, brushing with butter, and topping with fillings. Turn the bottom pastry ends up over the filling. Place the remaining eight pastry sheets smoothly over top, brushing each with butter. Using a spatula, tuck the top pastry sheets around the inside edges of the pan.
With a sharp knife, score the top lightly in half lengthwise and sixths crosswise (do not cut through). Bake in a moderate oven (350°F) for 1 hour or until the top is golden brown. Cool at least 10 minutes before cutting along the scored lines. Place a cherry tomato on each of 12 small wooden picks and insert the pick into the center of each serving. Garnish with parsley. Garnish individual servings with fresh fruit kabobs, if desired.
Fresh Fruit Kabobs: To make fresh fruit kabobs, place chunks of fresh pineapple, cantaloupe, whole strawberries, or other fruits on small wooden skewers.
